title: Pouteria acutangula
taxonomy: Pouteria acutangula is classified in the family Sapotaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the sapodilla or soapberry family. This family consists of about 100 genera and 1,000 species of trees and shrubs. Pouteria acutangula specifically belongs to the genus Pouteria, which comprises around 30 species of tropical trees and shrubs. The genus Pouteria is further divided into several sections, with Pouteria acutangula being part of the section Pouteria. The classification of Pouteria acutangula reflects its unique characteristics and relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Pouteria acutangula, also known as the Siam mangrove, is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae, commonly found in Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Pouteria acutangula include flavonoids, alkaloids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Pouteria acutangula is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is commonly used to treat various ailments, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections.
